entries.title: [Changelog] 2021-04-07: New Sizing Options for HCP Consul Standard Tier: Users have additional sizing options when deployin…
entries.summary:
New Sizing Options for HCP Consul Standard Tier: Users have additional sizing options when deploying a Consul Standard cluster. Medium and Large VM sizes are now available in HCP. See pricing

HCP Vault Generally Availability on AWS: HCP Vault gives you the power and security of HashiCorp Vault as a managed service. Read more

entries.title: [Changelog] 2021-05-03: Single Sign-On via Okta: HCP allows organizations to configure SAML 2.0 SSO (Single Sign-On) as an…
entries.summary: Single Sign-On via Okta: HCP allows organizations to configure SAML 2.0 SSO (Single Sign-On) as an alternative to traditional user management with GitHub and email-based options. This can help mitigate Account Take Over (ATO) attacks, provide a universal source of truth to federate identities from your identity provider (IDP), and better manage user access to your organization. At this time, HCP integrates with Okta as an identity provider, with others planned. Read more

entries.title: [Changelog] 2021-05-17: AWS Transit Gateway Attachments: As you grow your HCP footprint, you’ll need more elegant ways to s…
entries.summary:
AWS Transit Gateway Attachments: As you grow your HCP footprint, you’ll need more elegant ways to simplify networking at scale. That’s why we introduced support for transit gateway attachments in HCP. Transit gateways enable a “hub-and-spoke” configuration of your networks, a simpler and more secure option compared to the complexity of managing separate virtual private cloud (VPC) connections. Instead of establishing a VPC peering connection for every environment, you can create an “attachment” to a transit gateway. The transit gateway then manages these connections centrally. With a transit gateway, you can secure a single ingress/egress point, instead of monitoring multiple peering connections. To get started, read this tutorial and watch webinar.

HVN Routes (Web Portal Only): Users now have a single place to view/edit all of the connections associated with HVN’s. This includes the option for the user to view routes for BOTH peerings and TGW attachments. There are options to view/add/delete HVN routes through the UI. Read more

entries.title: [Changelog] 2021-06-07: HCP Terraform Provider 0.7.0: Version 0.7.0 of the HCP Terraform Provider is now available.

Note: This version contains breaking changes to the hcp_aws_transit_gateway_attachment and hcp_aws_network_peering resources and data sources. Please pin to the previous version and follow this migration guide when you're ready to migrate.

entries.summary: HCP Vault Admin Token Entity Policy Change: Users of HCP Vault admin tokens can now avoid unchecked client counts due to them not being associated with a Vault identity. This change attaches an entity to admin tokens, which results in the maximum admin-token associated client count for a cluster being 1 per month.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2022-06-30: HCP Packer no longer redacts the image identifier for revoked iterations: The image identifier imag…
entries.summary:
HCP Packer no longer redacts the image identifier for revoked iterations: The image identifier image_id will no longer be replaced with error_revoked for revoked iterations. Packer will continue to error when building new images from iterations that are revoked. Terraform will not error but users can validate iterations in their Terraform configurations to prevent new deployments of images from revoked iterations. Read more about validating iterations in Terraform

Note: This is a breaking change for Terraform Configurations that depends on error_revoked to validate iterations.

entries.summary: Platform Logs (HCP Consul): Platform Logs are auditable events across HCP. This will enable better tracking of changes within a customer's HCP Organization and meet troubleshooting and compliance needs. This release covers HCP Consul and tracks activity like cluster creation, upgrades and snapshots. These logs can be seen when navigating to a particular Consul cluster within the HCP Portal.

entries.summary: HCP Multi-project Support: HCP now supports multiple Projects within an HCP Organization. All new and existing Orgs have a default project created.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2024-01-30: HCP Packer New Nomenclature: Renaming of Image Buckets to Bucket: The prefix 'Image' has been remov…
entries.summary:
HCP Packer New Nomenclature:

  • Renaming of Image Buckets to Bucket:
    • The prefix 'Image' has been removed from all the resources. This change simplifies the naming convention and makes it more intuitive for users to identify and manage their resources.
  • Renaming of Iteration Resource to Version:
    • The 'Iteration' resource has been renamed to 'Version'. This change better reflects the functionality of the resource and aligns with standard terminology used in version control and software development practices.
  • Renaming of Image Resource to Artifact:
    • The 'Image' resource has been renamed to 'Artifact'. This renaming is part of our ongoing efforts to enhance clarity and consistency in our resource naming. 'Artifact' more accurately represents the nature and usage of these resources in the multi-cloud environment.

Notes: These changes are part of our commitment to improving user experience and aligning with industry standards. Version [0.82.0] of the HCP provider for Terraform reflects these changes. For any questions or concerns, please reach out to our support team.

In addition, HCP Boundary now supports the ability to manually upgrade clusters within a 30 day grace period of a new Boundary release. This can be set in the cluster configuration in the HCP Portal. After 30 days the upgrade will be performed automatically.

HCP unified audit log streaming: HCP's unified audit log streaming capabilities are now available as a public beta offering. You can use a new Terraform provider resource to send audit logs for platform and product events to one of the following external SIEMs: AWS Cloudwatch, Datadog, Splunk Cloud. For more information, refer to HCP audit log streaming.

Notes: The Secrets Sync feature which went GA in Vault 1.16 will remain disabled on HCP Azure clusters while we work on integrating it with the platform itself. Additionally, the Secrets Sync beta which was available in Vault 1.15 won't be available anymore after the upgrade to 1.16, which is why we're holding off on releasing 1.16 to AWS clusters until the integration work is completed.

entries.summary: HCP Vault 1.16.9 on Azure: Vault 1.16.9 is now available on HCP for Azure clusters. Refer to 1.16.9 Enterprise in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.16.9.

entries.summary:
HCP Vault 1.15.15 on AWS: Vault 1.15.15 is now available on HCP for AWS clusters. Refer to 1.15.15 Enterprise in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.15.15.

HCP Vault 1.16.10 on Azure: Vault 1.16.0 is now available on HCP for Azure clusters. Refer to 1.16.10 Enterprise in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.16.10.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2024-10-05: HCP Access Management: Project-level service principals can now be assigned access to multiple proj…
entries.summary: HCP Access Management: Project-level service principals can now be assigned access to multiple projects. This enables workflows that need to interact with more than one project at a time with varying levels of permissions. Refer to documentation for more details.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2024-10-14: HCP Audit Log Streaming : A public beta of HCP's unified audit log streaming capabilities is now av…
entries.summary: HCP Audit Log Streaming: A public beta of HCP's unified audit log streaming capabilities is now available. You can use a web-based UI workflow to send audit logs for your organization's platform and product events to one of the supported external SIEMs: AWS Cloudwatch, Datadog, or Splunk Cloud. For more information, refer to HCP audit log streaming.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2024-10-15: Vault Radar Public Beta: Vault Radar is currently available to be tested by any HCP user.
entries.summary:
Vault Radar Public Beta: Vault Radar is currently available to be tested by any HCP user. If you would like to get started, go to your HCP project and claim your Radar instance. Refer to Vault Radar product tiers to learn more.

HCP Vault 1.15.15 on AWS: Vault 1.15.15 is now available on HCP for AWS clusters. Refer to 1.15.15 Enterprise in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.15.15.

HCP Vault Secrets: Dynamic secrets for AWS and GCP are now available in public beta.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2024-10-24: HCP Access Management: The default soft limit that controls the concurrent number of projects withi…
entries.summary: HCP Access Management: The default soft limit that controls the concurrent number of projects within an HCP organization has now been raised to 100. This enables more unique use cases to be managed within an HCP organization and scale with an organization's needs. Refer to service quotas for more details.

entries.title:
[Changelog] 2024-11-11: HCP Vault 1.18.1 on AWS and Azure: Vault 1.18.1 has started rolling out to
HCP Vault Dedicated clus…
entries.summary:
HCP Vault 1.18.1 on AWS and Azure: Vault 1.18.1 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clusters on AWS and Azure. Refer to 1.18.1 Enterprise release notes in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.18.1.

Note

Workload identity federation (WIF) for HCP Vault Dedicated cluster's auth methods and secrets engines is not currently supported.

entries.summary: HCP Europe now available: With HCP Europe, your resources are hosted, managed, and billed separately to meet European data residency requirements. For more information, refer to HCP Europe.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2025-11-12: HCP Consul dedicated reached its End-of-Life (EOL) on November 12, 2025, and is no longer available.
entries.summary: HCP Consul dedicated reached its End-of-Life (EOL) on November 12, 2025, and is no longer available. All active clusters and associated project data were decommissioned in accordance with our EOL policy. For questions about your account and data, or for help migrating to a self-managed Consul deployment, contact our support team.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2025-11-27: HCP organizations now support the assignment of the Owner role to multiple users.
entries.summary:
HCP organizations now support the assignment of the Owner role to multiple users. This enhancement creates a more resilient administrative model, eliminating the business continuity risks and operational bottlenecks associated with single-principal ownership. For more information, refer to the access management documentation.

Key Update:

  • Multi-Owner Support: Owners can now delegate the Owner role to additional users, ensuring redundancy if another owner is unavailable.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2025-12-04: HCP Vault 1.20.6 on AWS and Azure: Vault 1.20.6 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clus…
entries.summary: HCP Vault 1.20.6 on AWS and Azure: Vault 1.20.6 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clusters on AWS and Azure. Refer to 1.20.6 Enterprise release notes in GitHub to learn more about what's new in 1.20.6.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2025-12-16: HCP Vault 1.21.1 on AWS and Azure: Vault 1.21.1 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clus…

entries.title: [Changelog] 2025-12-18: HCP Access Management: Project-level service principals can now be assigned organization-level role…
entries.summary:
HCP Access Management: Project-level service principals can now be assigned organization-level roles, such as Project Creator or Group Admin. This allows a single principal authenticated with workload identity federation (WIF) to securely automate organization-wide tasks without needing static, org-level keys. For more details, refer to the service principals documentation.

Key Update:

  • You can now grant organization-level permissions to project-level service principals, simplifying secure and large-scale automation.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2026-04-09: HCP Organizations: A self-service organization deletion checklist is now available for organization…
entries.summary: HCP Organizations: A self-service organization deletion checklist is now available for organization owners. The checklist provides an automated pre-flight check to verify that all prerequisites—such as decommissioning active resources, resolving billing requirements, and clearing IAM configurations—are met before an organization can be deleted. This reduces the need for manual support tickets and provides a more seamless, transparent experience. For more information, refer to the Organizations documentation.

entries.title: [Blog] Announcing the new Partner Premier tier for the Terraform Registry
entries.summary: HashiCorp is excited to announce the launch of a new Partner Premier status on the Terraform Registry.

entries.title: [Blog] SPIFFE: Securing the identity of agentic AI and non-human actors
entries.summary: With native SPIFFE auth support, Vault Enterprise simplifies and extends authentication of non-human-identity (NHI) workloads such as AI agents.

entries.title: [Blog] How Vault Secrets Operator (VSO) automates secret management for enterprises on Kubernetes
entries.summary: See how HashiCorp Vault Secrets Operator (VSO) perfects secret delivery in Kubernetes or Red Hat OpenShift to enable secure automated lifecycle management at scale.

entries.title: [Blog] Mitigate credential exposure in Windows environments with Boundary and Vault
entries.summary: Learn why dynamic credentials are critical to infrastructure access and how Boundary and Vault integrate with Windows AD DS to secure RDP connections.

entries.title: [Blog] Introducing HCP Terraform powered by Infragraph - now in public preview
entries.summary: Hybrid and multi-cloud estates create data silos. HCP Terraform powered by Infragraph provides a single source of truth to help optimize and secure infrastructure.

entries.title: [Blog] LDAP secrets management now available in IBM Vault Enterprise 2.0
entries.summary: Learn to migrate LDAP static roles to IBM Vault Enterprise 2.0’s centralized rotation system, featuring self-managed flows and automated lifecycle management.

entries.title: [Blog] Terraform adds cost visibility, project-level notifications, and more
entries.summary: HCP Terraform and Terraform Enterprise continue to reduce operational overhead and strengthen governance and security across the entire infrastructure lifecycle.

entries.title: [Blog] IBM Vault 2.0 adds UI enhancements and improved reporting visibility
entries.summary: IBM Vault 2.0 introduces UI enhancements, including in-product guidance to surface value faster, plus enhanced reporting for greater transparency and visibility.

entries.title: [Blog] Announcing native AI agent support in HashiCorp Vault
entries.summary: HashiCorp Vault now enables enterprises to manage agentic IAM including trusted identities, delegated authorization, fine-grained controls and end-to-end tracing.

entries.title: [Blog] Terraform Enterprise 2.0: Evolving infrastructure operations for scale
entries.summary: Terraform Enterprise 2.0 helps organizations scale infrastructure operations, strengthen governance, and reduce operational complexity.

entries.title: [Blog] New in Terraform 1.15: Dynamic sources, variable deprecation, and more
entries.summary: Terraform 1.15 adds Windows ARM64 builds, variable deprecation, S3 AWS login, inline type conversion, and dynamic module sources.

entries.title: [Blog] The great AI divide: Why early leaders embrace an AI operating model
entries.summary: The enterprises pulling ahead aren't experimenting with AI — they're operationalizing it. Here's what separates the leaders from the rest.

entries.title: [Blog] Azure hub-and-spoke generally available for HCP Vault Dedicated
entries.summary: Azure hub-and-spoke networking is now generally available in HCP Vault Dedicated, supporting enhanced cloud security maturity with private connectivity.

entries.title: [Blog] Encrypting large artifacts and streaming workloads with Vault
entries.summary: Use Vault Transit with envelope encryption to securely protect large artifacts and streaming workloads without sending payloads to Vault.

entries.title: [Blog] Consul 2.0 improves flexibility, control, and scalability
entries.summary: Consul 2.0 enhancements include multi-port for service mesh, CyberArk Workload Identity Manager, cluster rate limiting, and auto-scaling for API gateway.

entries.title: [Blog] SCIM in HashiCorp Vault standardizes provisioning in platforms
entries.summary: HashiCorp Vault 2.0 adds beta SCIM support, letting organizations standardize user and group provisioning from external identity platforms into Vault.

entries.title: [Blog] HCP Terraform adds project-level run tasks
entries.summary: HCP Terraform now supports project-level run tasks, enabling organizations to apply security, compliance, and operational guardrails consistently across groups of workspaces while reducing manual overhead.

entries.title: [Blog] Rethinking infrastructure access in the age of agentic AI
entries.summary: HashiCorp Boundary enables secure agentic AI at scale with unique identities, JIT credentials, explicit delegation, point-of-use enforcement, and auditable control.

entries.title: [Blog] With great AI power comes the need for zero trust responsibility
entries.summary: Learn how to mitigate autonomous AI exploits like Claude Mythos using IBM Vault Radar for preemptive hygiene and IBM Vault for dynamic runtime security.

entries.title: [Blog] HCP Packer adds enforced provisioners
entries.summary: HCP Packer now supports enforced provisioners, enabling platform and security teams to centrally apply mandatory provisioning steps across image builds.

entries.title: [Blog] Terraform MCP server is now generally available
entries.summary: Terraform MCP server 1.0 ensures consistent infrastructure across your organization with flexible deployment options.

entries.title: [Changelog] 2026-06-15: HCP Vault 2.0 for AWS and Azure: Vault 2.0 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clusters…
entries.summary: HCP Vault 2.0 for AWS and Azure: Vault 2.0 has started rolling out to HCP Vault Dedicated clusters on AWS and Azure. Refer to HCP Vault Dedicated changelog to learn more about Vault 2.0 capabilities available to HCP Vault.

entries.title: [Blog] Implementing workload identity with HashiCorp Vault and SPIFFE
entries.summary: Learn how to use HashiCorp Vault as a SPIFFE identity issuer and broker to streamline workload identity and authorization, while defining exactly where SPIRE fits
